methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pivotal is a Growth-stage transportation company founded by Marcus Leng and backed by Page. The broader transportation sector includes five identifiable private peers—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—though no public comparables exist for direct benchmarking.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this valuation. The Transportation sector peer set of five companies does not yield sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage matches with similar valuation tiers to support a reliable median multiple. Cross-sector median approaches would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of business models and capital intensity within transportation subsegments. The private peer universe, while informative for directional context, lacks the granularity needed for quantitative triangulation.

In the absence of applicable statistical methods, valuation of Pivotal requires alternative approaches not evident in the current dataset—such as discounted cash flow analysis with stage-specific discount rates, venture capital method benchmarking against growth-stage entry multiples in frontier tech, or transaction multiples from comparable rounds. The key uncertainty is the absence of standardized comparable data; resolution would require detailed financials (revenue, growth rate, burn profile) from both Pivotal and its peer set, as well as clarity on which transportation subsegment (autonomous vehicles, logistics infrastructure, or alternative mobility) most accurately defines the competitive set.
